raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The output identifies several internal contradictions and unsupported leaps in the paragraph, suggesting tightened wording and identifying claims that should be softened or removed because the reported evidence does not license them.",
    "R1.3": "The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding text, flagging at least one claim the evidence does not support.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., generalization to 7B–70B models and any language with a unit-test harness), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for every recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers most of the agreed J*(d) but drifts slightly by discussing model-agnostic signal without justifying it on n=1.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free but with one minor regression (generalization to 7B–70B models and any language with a unit-test harness).",
    "RX.5": "The output annotates every non-cosmetic change, but not always with the source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.6,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The output identifies several internal contradictions and unsupported leaps in the paragraph, suggesting tightened wording and identifying claims that should be softened or removed because the reported evidence does not license them.
rationale.R1.3: The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding text, flagging at least one claim the evidence does not support.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., generalization to 7B–70B models and any language with a unit-test harness), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for every recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers most of the agreed J*(d) but drifts slightly by discussing model-agnostic signal without justifying it on n=1.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free but with one minor regression (generalization to 7B–70B models and any language with a unit-test harness).
rationale.RX.5: The output annotates every non-cosmetic change, but not always with the source and reason.
